frameworks.name: generalized drift-diffusion model
frameworks.one_line: Sequential evidence accumulation extended to test suppression, pausing, or resetting variants.

frameworks.name: temporal integration of evidence
frameworks.one_line: Baseline framework for noisy stationary decisions, here challenged by non-stationarity.

frameworks.name: motor preparation arrest
frameworks.one_line: Brief suppression of motor output proposed as mechanism for handling evidence change.

core_question: How do decision processes adapt when sensory evidence is non-stationary and changes mid-trial?
key_assumptions: Decisions in stationary noise rely on temporal evidence integration
Frontal eye field neural activity reflects ongoing decision variables
Saccadic choice reports the accumulated evidence
manipulations: Dominant color of dynamically refreshed bicolor patch
Variable delay before evidence becomes informative
key_findings: Behavioral responses were briefly suppressed after evidence changes
Many frontal eye field neurons showed a corresponding activity dip after evidence change
The neural dip resembled post-stimulus-onset dips but was sensitive to stimulus strength
Model fits favored brief motor-output suppression over pausing or resetting integration
interpretations: Momentary arrest of motor preparation supports dynamic perceptual decision making
Evidence accumulation itself is neither paused nor reset by mid-trial changes
